title: Measurement, SI Units, Dimensional Analysis, and Uncertainty · Spiral Review
summary: Measure a room, select materials, convert specifications, and report tolerances for a community repair. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a unit-safe materials estimate after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose appropriate SI base and derived units and write their symbols correctly.
Convert quantities with unit factors that equal one and cancel dimensions explicitly.
Square or cube conversion factors when converting area and volume.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Unit Validation applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use dimensional consistency to detect formula and data-entry errors before calculation.
